title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
problem_statement: Given a sorted array, find two numbers that add up to a target and return their 1-based indices.
constraints: numbers is sorted in non-decreasing order.
intuition: Use a left and right pointer moving inward to find the target sum in the sorted array.

approaches.name: Two-pointer search
approaches.description: Start with pointers at both ends and adjust them based on whether the current sum is too low or too high.
approaches.why_not: Using a hash map is unnecessary given the sorted property.

interview_qa.q: What property makes two pointers effective here?
interview_qa.a: The input array is sorted, so moving pointers changes the sum predictably.

interview_qa.q: What happens when the sum is too large?
interview_qa.a: Move the right pointer left to reduce the sum.
